Integration tests for ParseError\n//!\n//! This test suite covers:\n//! - Error creation and formatting workflows\n//! - Error propagation through functions\n//! - Context building patterns\n//! - Multi-layer error scenarios\n//! - Integration with Result type\n\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, ParseErrorKind, Result};\nuse std::io;\n\n// ===========================================================================\n// Error Creation Workflows\n// ===========================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_complete_error_creation_workflow() {\n    // Simulate a typical error found during YAML parsing\n    let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid mapping syntax\")\n        .with_path(\"config/services.yaml\")\n        .with_location(15, 8)\n        .with_context(\"while parsing service definitions\")\n        .with_snippet(\"services:\\n  - name: web\\n    port: abc\");\n\n    // Verify all components are properly set\n    assert!(error.is_syntax());\n    assert_eq!(error.line, Some(15));\n    assert_eq!(error.column, Some(8));\n    assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config/services.yaml\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(error.context, \"while parsing service definitions\");\n    assert_eq!(error.snippet, Some(\"services:\\n  - name: web\\n    port: abc\".to_string()));\n\n    // Verify formatting produces useful output\n    let display = format!(\"{}\", error);\n    assert!(display.contains(\"config/services.yaml:15:8\"));\n    assert!(display.contains(\"syntax error: invalid mapping syntax\"));\n    assert!(display.contains(\"while parsing service definitions\"));\n    assert!(display.contains(\"services:\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_workflow_from_validation() {\n    // Simulate validation error with context\n    let port = 70000; // Invalid port number\n    let error = ParseError::validation(format!(\"port must be between 1 and 65535, got {}\", port))\n        .with_path(\"config/database.yaml\")\n        .with_location(25, 10)\n        .with_context(\"while validating database configuration\");\n\n    assert!(error.is_validation());\n    assert!(error.summary().contains(\"validation error\"));\n    assert!(error.summary().contains(\"port must be between 1 and 65535\"));\n    assert!(error.summary().contains(\"while validating database configuration\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_workflow_type_mismatch_nested() {\n    // Simulate type mismatch in nested structure\n    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"database.connection.timeout\", \"integer\", \"boolean\")\n        .with_path(\"production.yaml\")\n        .with_location(42, 18)\n        .with_context(\"while parsing database connection settings\")\n        .with_snippet(\"database:\\n  connection:\\n    timeout: true\");\n\n    assert!(error.is_type_mismatch());\n    let display = format!(\"{}\", error);\n    assert!(display.contains(\"database.connection.timeout\"));\n    assert!(display.contains(\"expected integer\"));\n    assert!(display.contains(\"got boolean\"));\n}\n\n// ===========================================================================\n// Error Propagation Patterns\n// ===========================================================================\n\nfn read_file_content(path: &str) -> Result<String> {\n    // Simulate file reading\n    Err(ParseError::io(\"file not found\")\n        .with_path(path)\n        .with_context(format!(\"while reading file: {}\", path)))\n}\n\nfn parse_yaml_content(content: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    // Simulate YAML parsing\n    Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML structure\")\n        .with_line(1)\n        .with_column(1)\n        .with_context(\"while parsing YAML document\"))\n}\n\nfn validate_config(value: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    // Simulate validation\n    Err(ParseError::validation(\"value must be positive\")\n        .with_line(10)\n        .with_context(\"while validating configuration\"))\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_propagation_io_to_parse_error() {\n    let result = read_file_content(\"config.yaml\");\n\n    match result {\n        Err(error) => {\n            assert!(error.is_io());\n            assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n            assert!(error.context.contains(\"while reading file\"));\n        }\n        Ok(_) => panic!(\"Expected error\"),\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_propagation_syntax_to_parse_error() {\n    let content = \"invalid: yaml: content:\";\n    let result = parse_yaml_content(content);\n\n    match result {\n        Err(error) => {\n            assert!(error.is_syntax());\n            assert_eq!(error.line, Some(1));\n            assert!(error.context.contains(\"while parsing YAML\"));\n        }\n        Ok(_) => panic!(\"Expected error\"),\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_propagation_validation_to_parse_error() {\n    let result = validate_config(\"-5\");\n\n    match result {\n        Err(error) => {\n            assert!(error.is_validation());\n            assert_eq!(error.line, Some(10));\n            assert!(error.context.contains(\"while validating\"));\n        }\n        Ok(_) => panic!(\"Expected error\"),\n    }\n}\n\n// ===========================================================================\n// Context Building Patterns\n// ===========================================================================\n\nfn parse_service_config(value: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    // Multi-layer error with nested context\n    Err(ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n        .with_line(5)\n        .with_column(10)\n        .with_context(\"while parsing service configuration\")\n        .with_snippet(\"service:\\n  name: web\\n  port: \\\"80\\\"\"))\n}\n\nfn parse_database_config(value: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    // Context with file information\n    Err(ParseError::validation(\"host cannot be empty\")\n        .with_path(\"config/database.yaml\")\n        .with_line(12)\n        .with_context(\"while parsing database connection string\")\n        .with_snippet(\"database:\\n  host: \\\"\\\"\"))\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_context_building_pattern_service() {\n    let result = parse_service_config(\"test\");\n\n    match result {\n        Err(error) => {\n            assert!(error.is_type_mismatch());\n            assert!(error.context.contains(\"while parsing service configuration\"));\n            assert!(error.snippet.is_some());\n            let snippet = error.snippet.unwrap();\n            assert!(snippet.contains(\"port: \\\"80\\\"\"));\n        }\n        Ok(_) => panic!(\"Expected error\"),\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_context_building_pattern_database() {\n    let result = parse_database_config(\"test\");\n\n    match result {\n        Err(error) => {\n            assert!(error.is_validation());\n            assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config/database.yaml\".to_string()));\n            assert!(error.context.contains(\"while parsing database connection string\"));\n            assert!(error.summary().contains(\"host cannot be empty\"));\n        }\n        Ok(_) => panic!(\"Expected error\"),\n    }\n}\n\n// ===========================================================================\n// Multi-Layer Error Scenarios\n// ===========================================================================\n\nfn process_config_file(path: &str) -> Result<()> {\n    // Layer 1: File reading\n    let content = read_file_content(path)?;\n\n    // Layer 2: YAML parsing\n    parse_yaml_content(&content)?;\n\n    // Layer 3: Validation\n    validate_config(&content)?;\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_multi_layer_error_propagation() {\n    let result = process_config_file(\"nonexistent.yaml\");\n\n    match result {\n        Err(error) => {\n            // Should fail at first layer (file reading)\n            assert!(error.is_io());\n            assert!(error.context.contains(\"while reading file\"));\n        }\n        Ok(_) => panic!(\"Expected error at file reading layer\"),\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_accumulation_workflow() {\n    // Simulate multiple errors in a single document\n    let errors = vec![\n        ParseError::syntax(\"invalid indentation\")\n            .with_line(5)\n            .with_column(4)\n            .with_path(\"config.yaml\"),\n        ParseError::type_mismatch(\"port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n            .with_line(10)\n            .with_column(12)\n            .with_path(\"config.yaml\"),\n        ParseError::validation(\"port out of range\")\n            .with_line(10)\n            .with_column(12)\n            .with_path(\"config.yaml\"),\n    ];\n\n    assert_eq!(errors.len(), 3);\n\n    // Verify each error\n    assert!(errors[0].is_syntax());\n    assert!(errors[1].is_type_mismatch());\n    assert!(errors[2].is_validation());\n\n    // Verify all have the same path\n    assert_eq!(errors[0].path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(errors[1].path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(errors[2].path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n}\n\n// ===========================================================================\n// Error Formatting Integration\n// ===========================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_report_generation_workflow() {\n    let error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid escape sequence\")\n        .with_path(\"config/app.yaml\")\n        .with_location(8, 25)\n        .with_context(\"while parsing application name\")\n        .with_snippet(\"app:\\n  name: \\\"My\\\\nApp\\\"\");\n\n    // Generate different report formats\n    let summary = error.summary();\n    let detailed = error.detailed_report();\n    let structured = error.format_structured();\n    let display = format!(\"{}\", error);\n    let debug = format!(\"{:?}\", error);\n\n    // Verify each format contains expected information\n    assert!(summary.contains(\"config/app.yaml:8:25\"));\n    assert!(summary.contains(\"syntax error: invalid escape sequence\"));\n    assert!(summary.contains(\"while parsing application name\"));\n\n    assert!(detailed.contains(\"error:\"));\n    assert!(detailed.contains(\"context:\"));\n    assert!(detailed.contains(\"snippet:\"));\n    assert!(detailed.contains(\"My\\\\nApp\"));\n\n    assert!(structured.contains(\"ParseError\"));\n    assert!(structured.contains(\"config/app.yaml:8:25\"));\n    assert!(structured.contains(\"line: Some(8)\"));\n\n    assert!(display.contains(\"config/app.yaml:8:25\"));\n    assert!(display.contains(\"syntax error\"));\n\n    assert!(debug.contains(\"ParseError\"));\n    assert!(debug.contains(\"kind\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_logging_workflow() {\n    let errors = vec![\n        ParseError::io(\"permission denied\").with_path(\"/etc/config.yaml\"),\n        ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\").with_path(\"user.yaml\").with_line(10),\n        ParseError::validation(\"invalid port\").with_path(\"service.yaml\").with_line(5),\n    ];\n\n    // Simulate logging each error\n    let log_entries: Vec<String> = errors\n        .iter()\n        .map(|e| e.summary())\n        .collect();\n\n    assert_eq!(log_entries.len(), 3);\n    assert!(log_entries[0].contains(\"I/O error\"));\n    assert!(log_entries[1].contains(\"syntax error\"));\n    assert!(log_entries[2].contains(\"validation error\"));\n}\n\n// ===========================================================================\n// Result Type Integration\n// ===========================================================================\n\nfn returns_result_ok() -> Result<String> {\n    Ok(\"success\".to_string())\n}\n\nfn returns_result_err() -> Result<String> {\n    Err(ParseError::validation(\"failed validation\"))\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_result_type_integration_ok() {\n    let result = returns_result_ok();\n    assert!(result.is_ok());\n    assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), \"success\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_result_type_integration_err() {\n    let result = returns_result_err();\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n\n    match result {\n        Ok(_) => panic!(\"Expected error\"),\n        Err(error) => {\n            assert!(error.is_validation());\n            // The error message is in the kind, not context\n            assert!(error.to_string().contains(\"failed validation\"));\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_result_type_with_question_operator() {\n    fn function_with_question() -> Result<String> {\n        let value = returns_result_ok()?;\n        Ok(value)\n    }\n\n    let result = function_with_question();\n    assert!(result.is_ok());\n    assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), \"success\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_result_type_with_question_operator_error() {\n    fn function_with_question() -> Result<String> {\n        let value = returns_result_err()?;\n        Ok(value)\n    }\n\n    let result = function_with_question();\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n\n    match result {\n        Err(error) => {\n            assert!(error.is_validation());\n        }\n        Ok(_) => panic!(\"Expected error\"),\n    }\n}\n\n// ===========================================================================\n// Real-World Error Scenarios\n// assert!(display.contains(\"config/production.yaml\"));\n    assert!(display.contains(\"I/O error\"));\n    assert!(display.contains(\"while loading configuration file\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_real_world_scenario_invalid_yaml_syntax() {\n    let error = ParseError::syntax(\"found unexpected ':' while parsing a flow sequence\")\n        .with_path(\"values.yaml\")\n   assert!(summary.contains(\"duplicate key: name\"));\n    assert!(summary.contains(\"duplicate service name found\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_real_world_scenario_unexpected_eof() {\n    let error = code, this would use the From trait implementation\n    let parse_error = ParseError::io(io_error.to_string())\n        .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n        .with_context(\"while reading configuration Simulate a custom error type conversion\n    enum CustomError {\n        InvalidConfig(String),\n        MissingField(String),\n    }\n\n    let custom_error = CustomError::InvalidConfig(\"port value is invalid\".to_string());\n\n    let parse_error = match custom_error {\n        CustomError::InvalidConfig(msg) => {\n            ParseError::validation(msg)\n                .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n                .with_line(10)\n                .with_context(\"while validating custom configuration\")\n        }\n        CustomError::MissingField(field) => {\n            ParseError::validation(format!(\"missing required field: {}\", field))\n                .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n                .with_context(\"while checking required fields\")\n        }\n    };\n\n    assert!(parse_error.is_validation());\n    assert!(parse_error.context.contains(\"while validating custom configuration\"));\n}\n\n// test_complex_error_multiple_issues() {\n    // Simulate a file with multiple related errors\n    let content = r#\"services:\n  - name: web\n    port: \"8080\"\n  - name: database\n    port: abc\n    host: localhost\"#;\n\n    let mut errors = Vec::new();\n\n    // Error 1: Type mismatch for first service port\n    errors.push(\n        ParseError::type_mismatch(\"services[0].port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n            .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n            .with_line(3)\n            .with_column(10)\n            .with_snippet(content)\n    );\n\n    // Error 2: Type mismatch for second service port\n    errors.push(\n        ParseError::type_mismatch(\"services[1].port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n            .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n            .with_line(5)\n            .with_column(10)\n            .with_snippet(content)\n    );\n\n    assert_eq!(errors.len(), 2);\n    assert!(errors.iter().all(|e| e.is_type_mismatch()));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_complex_error_with_chained_context() {\n    // Simulate error passing through multiple layers\n    let base_error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid escape sequence\");\n\n    let level1 = base_error\n        .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n        .with_line(10)\n        .with_column(15);\n\n    let level2 = level1\n        .with_context(\"while parsing string value\");\n\n    let level3 = level2\n        .with_snippet(\"value: \\\"test\\\\nstring\\\"\")\n        .with_context(\"while parsing service configuration\");\n\n    // All layers preserved\n    assert_eq!(level3.path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(level3.line, Some(10));\n    assert_eq!(level3.column, Some(15));\n    assert_eq!(level3.snippet, Some(\"value: continuing after an error\n    let results: Vec<Result<String>> = vec![\n        Err(ParseError::syntax(\"error 1\").with_line(1)),\n        Err(ParseError::validation(\"error 2\").with_line(2)),\n        Err(ParseError::io(\"error 3\").with_line(3)),\n    ];\n\n    let errors: Vec<ParseError> = results\n        .into_iter()\n        .filter_map(|r| r.err())\n        .collect();\n\n Integration tests for ParseError error propagation\n//!\n//! This test suite demonstrates error propagation through a call stack using\n//! the Result<T, ParseError> pattern and the ? operator.\n\nuse armor::parsers::yaml::{ParseError, Result};\nuse std::io;\nuse std::path::Path;\n\n/// Simulates reading a configuration file\nfn read_config_file(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {\n    // This will fail since the file doesn't exist\n    // The io::Error automatically converts to ParseError via the From impl\n    let content = std::fs::read_to_string(path)?;\n    Ok(content)\n}\n\n/// Simulates parsing YAML content\nfn parse_yaml_content(content: &str) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n    // This will fail if the YAML is invalid\n    // serde_yaml::Error automatically converts to ParseError via the From impl\n    let value = serde_yaml::from_str(content)?;\n    Ok(value)\n}\n\n/// Simulates validating a specific field from the parsed YAML\nfn extract_database_config(value: &serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<DatabaseConfig> {\n    // Extract database configuration with context\n    let db_section = value.get(\"database\")\n        .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::validation(\"missing 'database' section\")\n            .with_context(\"while extracting database configuration\"))?;\n\n    let host = db_section.get(\"host\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n        .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"database.host\", \"string\", \"null\")\n            .with_context(\"while extracting database host\"))?;\n\n    let port = db_section.get(\"port\")\n        .and_then(|v| v.as_i64())\n        .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"database.port\", \"integer\", \"null\")\n            .with_context(\"while extracting database port\"))?;\n\n    Ok(DatabaseConfig {\n        host: host.to_string(),\n        port: port as u16,\n    })\n}\n\n/// Simulates a complete configuration loading workflow\nfn load_config_from_file(path: &Path) -> Result<DatabaseConfig> {\n    // This demonstrates error propagation through a call stack:\n    // 1. read_config_file can fail with io::Error → ParseError\n    // 2. parse_yaml_content can fail with serde_yaml::Error → ParseError\n    // 3. extract_database_config can fail with custom ParseError\n\n    let content = read_config_file(path)?;\n    let yaml = parse_yaml_content(&content)?;\n    let config = extract_database_config(&yaml)?;\n\n    Ok(config)\n}\n\n#[derive(Debug)]\nstruct DatabaseConfig {\n    host: String,\n    port: u16,\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_result_parse_error_compiles() {\n    // Test that Result<T, ParseError> compiles and works correctly\n    fn returns_parse_error() -> Result<String> {\n        Ok(\"success\".to_string())\n    }\n\n    let result: Result<String> = returns_parse_error();\n    assert!(result.is_ok());\n    assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), \"success\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_from_io_error() {\n    // Test that std::io::Error converts to ParseError\n    let io_err = io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::NotFound, \"file not found\");\n    let parse_err: ParseError = io_err.into();\n\n    assert!(parse_err.is_io());\n    assert!(parse_err.to_string().contains(\"file not found\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_from_serde_yaml_error() {\n    // Test that serde_yaml::Error converts to ParseError\n    let invalid_yaml = \"not: valid: yaml: [\";\n    let serde_err = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(invalid_yaml).unwrap_err();\n    let parse_err: ParseError = serde_err.into();\n\n    // Should classify as an error assert!(!parse_err.context.is_empty());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_from_from_utf8_error() {\n    // Test that std::string::FromUtf8Error converts to ParseError\n    let invalid_bytes = vec![0xFF, 0xFE, UTF-8\"));\n    assert!(!parse_err.context.is_empty());\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_propagation_with_question_mark() {\n    // Test error propagation through call stack with ? operator\n    let test_error_creation_from_yaml_parsing_context() {\n38\t    /// Simulates YAML parsing with syntax error detection\n39\t    fn parse_yaml_with_context(content: &str, source_path: &str) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n40\t        serde_yaml::from_str(content)\n41\t            .map_err(|e| {\n42\t                let parse_err = ParseError::from(e);\n43\t                parse_err\n44\t                    .with_path(source_path.to_string())\n45\t                    .with_context(\"while parsing YAML document structure\")\n46\t            })\n47\t    }\n48\t\n49\t    let invalid_yaml = checking\n63\t    fn validate_port_field(value: &serde_yaml::Value, field_path: &str) -> Result<u16> {\n64\t        value.as_i64()\n65\t            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\n66\t                field_path,\n67\t                \"integer\",\n68\t                value.as_str().unwrap_or(\"null\")\n69\t            ).with_context(\"while validating port configuration\"))\n70\t            .and_then(|port| {\n71\t                if port < 1 || port > 65535 {\n72\t                    Err(ParseError::validation(\n73\t                        format!(\"port {} is out of valid range (1-65535)\", port)\n74\t                    ).with_context(\"while validating port configuration\"))\n75\t                } else {\n76\t                    Ok(port as u16)\n77\t                }\n78\t            })\n79\t    }\n80\t\n81\t    // Test type mismatch error creation\n82\t    let invalid_value = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(\"port: \\\"8080\\\"\").unwrap();\n83\t    let test_error_creation_from_nested_parsing_context() {\n95\t    /// Simulates nested structure parsing with error accumulation\n96\t    fn extract_nested_field(value: &serde_yaml::Value, path: &str) -> Result<String> {\n97\t        let parts: Vec<&str> = path.split('.').collect();\n98\t        let mut current = value;\n99\t\n100\t        for (i, part) in parts.iter().enumerate() {\n101\t            match current.get(part) {\n102\t                Some(next) => current = next,\n103\t                None => {\n104\t                    return Err(ParseError::validation(\n105\t                        format!(\"missing field: {}\", part)\n106\t                    )\n107\t                    .with_context(format!(\n108\t                        \"while navigating nested structure at level {} (path: {})\",\n109\t                        i,\n110\t                        path\n111\t                    )))\n112\t                }\n113\t            }\n114\t        }\n115\t\n116\t        current.as_str()\n117\t            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n118\t            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\n119\t                path,\n120\t                \"string\",\n121\t                \"null or other type\"\n122\t            ).with_context(format!(\"while extracting final value from path: {}\", path)))\n123\t    }\n124\t\n125\t    let yaml = r#\"database:\n126\t  connection:\n127\t    timeout: 30\"#;\n128\t    let value = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(yaml).unwrap();\n129\t\n130\t    // Test successful extraction\n131\t    let result = extract_nested_field(&value, \"database.connection.timeout\");\n132\t    assert!(result.is_err()); // timeout is a number, not string\n133\t\n134\t    // Test missing field error\n135\t    let result = extract_nested_field(&value, \"database.connection.missing\");\n136\t    assert!(result.is_err());\n137\t    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n138\t    assert!(error.context.contains(\"while navigating nested structure\"));\n139\t    assert!(error.context.contains(\"level\"));\n140\t}\n141\t\n142\t// ===========================================================================\n143\t// ===========================================================================\n272\t// Error Propagation Through Result Types\n273\t// }\n286\t\n287\t    fn validate_config(value: serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n288\t        // Simulate validation\n289\t        if value.get(\"required_field\").is_none() {\n290\t            return Err(ParseError::validation(\"missing required field: required_field\")\n291\t                .with_context(\"while validating configuration structure\"));\n292\t        }\n293\t        Ok(value)\n294\t    }\n295\t\n296\t    fn full_pipeline(path: &Path) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n297\t        let content = read_file(path)?;\n298\t        let yaml = parse_yaml(&content)?;\n299\t        let validated = validate_config(yaml)?;\n300\t        Ok(validated)\n301\t    }\n302\t\n303\t    // Test error propagation at file reading stage\n304\t    let result = file\"));\n308\t}\n309\t\n310\t#[test]\n311\tfn test_error_propagation_with_context_accumulation() {\n312\t    fn level_3_operation() -> Result<String> {\n313\t        Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid Result<String> {\n320\t        level_3_operation()\n321\t            .map_err(|e| e.with_context(\"while processing statement\"))\n322\t    }\n323\t\n324\t    fn level_1_operation() -> Result<String> {\n325\t        level_2_operation()\n326\t            .map_err(|e| e.with_context(\"while parsing function body\"))\n327\t    }\n328\t\n329\t    let result = level_1_operation();\n330\t    assert!(result.is_err());\n331\t\n332\t    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n333\t    // Context should be accumulated through the chain\n334\t    assert!(error.context.contains(\"while parsing test_error_propagation_with_successful_intermediate_steps() {\n341\t    fn step1_read() -> Result<String> {\n342\t        Ok(\"valid: content\".to_string())\n343\t    }\n344\t\n345\t    fn step2_parse(content: String) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n346\t        serde_yaml::from_str(&content)\n347\t            .map_err(|e| ParseError::from(e).with_context(\"while parsing YAML\"))\n348\t    }\n349\t\n350\t    fn step3_validate(value: serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<String> {\n351\t        value.get(\"valid\")\n352\t            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n353\t            .map(|s| }\n357\t\n358\t    let result = step1_read()\n359\t        .and_then(|content| step2_parse(content))\n360\t        .and_then(|value| step3_validate(value));\n361\t\n362\t test_error_conversion_from_utf8_error() {\n439\t    fn parse_yaml_bytes(bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n440\t        let content = std::str::from_utf8(bytes)?; // Utf8Error automatically converts\n441\t        serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content)?; // serde_yaml::Error automatically converts\n442\t        Ok(serde_yaml::Value::Null)\n443\t    }\n444\t\n445\t    let complex_parsing_operation(bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<String> {\n457\t        // Step 1: UTF-8 conversion (Utf8Error → ParseError)\n458\t        let content = std::str::from_utf8(bytes)?;\n459\t\n460\t        // Step 2: YAML parsing (serde_yaml::Error → ParseError)\n461\t        let value = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content)?;\n462\t\n463\t        // Step 3: Field extraction (custom ParseError)\n464\t        let field = value.get(\"target\")\n465\t            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::validation(\"missing 'target' field\"))?;\n466\t\n467\t        let string_value = field.as_str()\n468\t            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"target\", \"string\", \"other\"))?;\n469\t\n470\t        Ok(string_value.to_string())\n471\t    }\n472\t\n473\t    // Test UTF-8 error conversion\n474\t    let result = complex_parsing_operation(b\"\\xFF target: test\");\n475\t    assert!(result.is_err());\n476\t    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n477\t    assert!(error.to_string().contains(\"invalid UTF-8\"));\n478\t\n479\t    // Test YAML parsing error conversion\n480\t    let result = complex_parsing_operation(b\"invalid: yaml: [\");\n481\t    assert!(result.is_err());\n482\t    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n483\t    assert!(error.to_string().contains(\"error\") || error.to_string().contains(\"syntax\"));\n484\t\n485\t    // Test custom error creation\n486\t    let result = complex_parsing_operation(b\"other: value\");\n487\t    assert!(result.is_err());\n488\t    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n489\t    assert!(error.is_validation());\n490\t    assert!(error.to_string().contains(\"missing 'target'\"));\n491\t}\n492\t\n493\t// ===========================================================================\n494\t// Error Context Preservation\n495\t// ===========================================================================\n496\t\n497\t#[test]\n498\tfn test_error_context_preservation_through_multiple_layers() {\n499\t    fn inner_parser() -> Result<String> {\n500\t        Err(ParseError::syntax(\"unexpected token\")\n501\t            .with_line(5)\n502\t            .with_column(10)\n503\t            .with_snippet(\"value: unexpected_token\"))\n504\t    }\n505\t\n506\t    fn middle_processor() -> Result<String> {\n507\t        inner_parser()\n508\t            .map_err(|e| e\n509\t                .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n510\t                .with_context(\"while processing configuration file\"))\n511\t    }\n512\t\n513\t    fn outer_validator() -> Result<String> {\n514\t        middle_processor()\n515\t            .map_err(|e| e\n516\t                .with_context(\"while validating application configuration\")\n517\t                .with_context(\"during startup validation\"))\n518\t    }\n519\t\n520\t    let result = outer_validator();\n521\t    assert!(result.is_err());\n522\t\n523\t    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n524\t\n525\t    // Original context should be from the outermost layer\n533\t    assert!(error.context.contains(\"during startup validation\") ||\n534\t            error.context.contains(\"while validating\") ||\n535\t \\\"My\\\\nApp\\\"\".to_string()));\n553\t    assert_eq!(enhanced_error.path, Some(\"config/app.yaml\".to_string()));\n554\t    assert_eq!(enhanced_error.context, \"while parsing application vec![\n561\t            ParseError::io(\"file not found\")\n562\t                .with_path(\"config1.yaml\")\n563\t                .with_context(\"while loading first config\"),\n564\t and converting I/O errors\n    fn read_config_file(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {\n        std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n            .map_err(|e| ParseError::io(e.to_string())\n                .with_path(path.to_string_lossy().to_string())\n                .with_context(format!(\"while reading configuration file: {}\", path.display())))\n    }\n\n    let result = parse_yaml_with_context(content: &str, source_path: &str) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        serde_yaml::from_str(content)\n            .map_err(|e| {\n                let parse_err = ParseError::from(e);\n                parse_err\n                    .with_path(source_path.to_string())\n                    .with_context(\"while parsing YAML document structure\")\n            })\n    }\n\n    let invalid_yaml = \"invalid: yaml: [unclosed\";\n    let result = parse_yaml_with_context(invalid_yaml, \"config/services.yaml\");\n\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = field_path: &str) -> Result<u16> {\n        value.as_i64()\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\n                field_path,\n                \"integer\",\n                value.as_str().unwrap_or(\"null\")\n            ).with_context(\"while validating port configuration\"))\n            .and_then(|port| {\n                if port < 1 || port > 65535 {\n   fn extract_nested_field(value: &serde_yaml::Value, path: &str) -> Result<String> {\n        let parts: Vec<&str> = path.split('.').collect();\n        let mut current = value;\n\n        for (i, part) in parts.iter().enumerate() {\n            match current.get(part) {\n                Some(next) => current = next,\n                None => {\n                    return Err(ParseError::validation(\n                        format!(\"missing field: {}\", part)\n                    )\n                    .with_context(format!(\n                        \"while navigating nested structure at level {} (path: {})\",\n                        i,\n                        path\n                    )))\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        current.as_str()\n            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\n                path,\n                \"string\",\n                \"null or other type\"\n            ).with_context(format!(\"while extracting final value from path: {}\", path)))\n    }\n\n    let yaml = r#\"database:\n  connection:\n    timeout: 30\"#;\n    let value = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(yaml).unwrap();\n\n    // Test successful extraction\n    let result = extract_nested_field(&value, \"database.connection.timeout\");\n    assert!(result.is_err()); // timeout is a number, not string\n\n    // Test missing field error\n    let result = extract_nested_field(&value, \"database.connection.missing\");\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(error.context.contains(\"while navigating nested structure\"));\n    assert!(error.context.contains(\"level\"));\n}\n\n// ===========================================================================\n// Error Propagation Through Result Types\n// ===========================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_propagation_through_parsing_pipeline() {\n    fn read_file(path: &Path) -> Result<String> {\n        std::fs::read_to_string(path)\n            .map_err(|e| ParseError::from(e).with_context(\"while reading file\"))\n    }\n\n    fn parse_yaml(content: &str) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        serde_yaml::from_str(content)\n            .map_err(|e| ParseError::from(e).with_context(\"while parsing YAML\"))\n    }\n\n    fn validate_config(value: serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Simulate validation\n        if value.get(\"required_field\").is_none() {\n            return Err(ParseError::validation(\"missing required field: required_field\")\n                .with_context(\"while validating configuration structure\"));\n        }\n        Ok(value)\n    }\n\n    fn full_pipeline(path: &Path) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        let content = read_file(path)?;\n        let yaml = parse_yaml(&content)?;\n        let validated = validate_config(yaml)?;\n        Ok(validated)\n    }\n\n    // Test error propagation at file reading stage\n    let result = full_pipeline(Path::new(\"/nonexistent/config.yaml\"));\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(error.context.contains(\"while reading file\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_propagation_with_context_accumulation() {\n    fn level_3_operation() -> Result<String> {\n        Err(ParseError::syntax(\"invalid token\")\n            .with_line(10)\n            .with_column(5)\n            .with_context(\"while parsing token\"))\n    }\n\n    fn level_2_operation() -> Result<String> {\n        level_3_operation()\n            .map_err(|e| e.with_context(\"while processing statement\"))\n    }\n\n    fn level_1_operation() -> Result<String> {\n        level_2_operation()\n            .map_err(|e| e.with_context(\"while parsing function body\"))\n    }\n\n    let result = level_1_operation();\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    // Context should be accumulated through the chain\n    assert!(error.context.contains(\"while parsing function body\"));\n    assert!(error.line == Some(10)); // Original line preserved\n    assert!(error.column == Some(5)); // Original column preserved\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_propagation_with_successful_intermediate_steps() {\n    fn step1_read() -> Result<String> {\n        Ok(\"valid: content\".to_string())\n    }\n\n    fn step2_parse(content: String) -> Result<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        serde_yaml::from_str(&content)\n            .map_err(|e| ParseError::from(e).with_context(\"while parsing YAML\"))\n    }\n\n    fn step3_validate(value: serde_yaml::Value) -> Result<String> {\n        value.get(\"valid\")\n            .and_then(|v| v.as_str())\n            .map(|s| s.to_string())\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"valid\", \"string\", \"null\")\n                .with_context(\"while extracting 'valid' field\"))\n    }\n\n    let result = step1_read()\n        .and_then(|content| step2_parse(content))\n        .and_then(|value| step3_validate(value));\n\n    assert!(result.is_ok());\n    assert_eq!(result.unwrap(), \"content\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_propagation_with_question_operator() {\n    fn multi_step_pipeline(input: &str) -> Result<String> {\n        let parsed = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(input)?;\n        let field = parsed.get(\"field\")\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::validation(\"missing 'field'\"))?;\n        let value = field.as_str()\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"field\", \"string\", \"null\"))?;\n        Ok(value.to_string())\n    }\n\n    // Test with invalid YAML\n    let result = multi_step_pipeline(\"invalid: yaml: [\");\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    // Error should be from serde_yaml parsing\n    assert!(error.is_syntax() || error.to_string().contains(\"error\"));\n\n    // Test with valid YAML but missing field\n    let result = multi_step_pipeline(\"other: value\");\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(error.is_validation());\n    assert!(error.to_string().contains(\"missing 'field'\"));\n\n    // Test with wrong type\n    let result = multi_step_pipeline(\"field: 123\");\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(error.is_type_mismatch());\n}\n\n// serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content)?; // serde_yaml::Error automatically converts\n        Ok(serde_yaml::Value::Null)\n    }\n\n    let invalid_yaml = \"unclosed: [\";\n    let result = parse_yaml_content(invalid_yaml);\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    // Should be converted from serde_yaml::Error\n    assert!(!error.to_string().is_empty());\n\n    // Error should contain useful information\n    let error_string = error.to_string();\n    assert!(error_string.contains(\"error\") || Ok(serde_yaml::Value::Null)\n    }\n\n    let invalid_utf8 = b\"\\xFF\\xFE invalid\";\n    let result = parse_yaml_bytes(invalid_utf8);\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    // Should be converted from Utf8Error\n    assert!(error.to_string().contains(\"invalid UTF-8\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_conversion_chain() {\n    fn complex_parsing_operation(bytes: &[u8]) -> Result<String> {\n        // Step 1: UTF-8 conversion (Utf8Error → ParseError)\n        let content = std::str::from_utf8(bytes)?;\n\n        // Step 2: YAML parsing (serde_yaml::Error → ParseError)\n        let value = serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content)?;\n\n        // Step 3: Field extraction (custom ParseError)\n        let field = value.get(\"target\")\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::validation(\"missing 'target' field\"))?;\n\n        let string_value = field.as_str()\n            .ok_or_else(|| ParseError::type_mismatch(\"target\", \"string\", \"other\"))?;\n\n        Ok(string_value.to_string())\n    }\n\n    // Test UTF-8 error conversion\n    let result = complex_parsing_operation(b\"\\xFF target: test\");\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(error.to_string().contains(\"invalid UTF-8\"));\n\n    // Test YAML parsing error conversion\n    let result = complex_parsing_operation(b\"invalid: yaml: [\");\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(error.to_string().contains(\"error\") || error.to_string().contains(\"syntax\"));\n\n    // Test custom error creation\n    let result = complex_parsing_operation(b\"other: value\");\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n    assert!(error.is_validation());\n    assert!(error.to_string().contains(\"missing 'target'\"));\n}\n\n// ===========================================================================\n// Error Context Preservation\n// ===========================================================================\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_context_preservation_through_multiple_layers() {\n    fn inner_parser() -> Result<String> {\n        Err(ParseError::syntax(\"unexpected token\")\n            .with_line(5)\n            .with_column(10)\n            .with_snippet(\"value: unexpected_token\"))\n    }\n\n    fn middle_processor() -> Result<String> {\n        inner_parser()\n            .map_err(|e| e\n                .with_path(\"config.yaml\")\n                .with_context(\"while processing configuration file\"))\n    }\n\n    fn outer_validator() -> Result<String> {\n        middle_processor()\n            .map_err(|e| e\n                .with_context(\"while validating application configuration\")\n                .with_context(\"during startup validation\"))\n    }\n\n    let result = outer_validator();\n    assert!(result.is_err());\n\n    let error = result.unwrap_err();\n\n    // Original context should be preserved\n    assert_eq!(error.line, Some(5));\n    assert_eq!(error.column, Some(10));\n    assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"config.yaml\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(error.snippet, Some(\"value: unexpected_token\".to_string()));\n\n    // Context should be accumulated (note: current implementation overwrites)\n    // The final context should be from the outermost layer\n    assert!(error.context.contains(\"during startup validation\") ||\n            error.context.contains(\"while validating\") ||\n            error.context.contains(\"while processing\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_context_preservation_with_snippets() {\n    let original_error = ParseError::syntax(\"invalid escape sequence\")\n        .with_line(8)\n        .with_column(15)\n        .with_snippet(\"app:\\n  name: \\\"My\\\\nApp\\\"\");\n\n    let enhanced_error = original_error\n        .with_path(\"config/app.yaml\")\n        .with_context(\"while parsing application configuration\");\n\n    // Verify all context is preserved\n    assert_eq!(enhanced_error.line, Some(8));\n    assert_eq!(enhanced_error.column, Some(15));\n    assert_eq!(enhanced_error.snippet, Some(\"app:\\n  name: \\\"My\\\\nApp\\\"\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(enhanced_error.path, Some(\"config/app.yaml\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(enhanced_error.context, \"while parsing application configuration\");\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_context_preservation_in_collection() {\n    fn collect_errors_from_multiple_sources() -> Vec<ParseError> {\n        vec![\n            ParseError::io(\"file not found\")\n                .with_path(\"config1.yaml\")\n                .with_context(\"while loading first config\"),\n            ParseError::syntax(\"invalid YAML\")\n                .with_path(\"config2.yaml\")\n                .with_line(10)\n                .with_context(\"while parsing second config\"),\n            ParseError::validation(\"port out of range\")\n                .with_path(\"config3.yaml\")\n                .with_line(5)\n                .with_snippet(\"port: 70000\")\n                .with_context(\"while validating third config\"),\n        ]\n    }\n\n    let errors = collect_errors_from_multiple_sources();\n\n    // Verify each error preserves its unique context\n    assert_eq!(errors.len(), 3);\n\n    assert!(errors[0].is_io());\n    assert_eq!(errors[0].path, Some(\"config1.yaml\".to_string()));\n    assert!(errors[0].context.contains(\"while loading first config\"));\n\n    assert!(errors[1].is_syntax());\n    assert_eq!(errors[1].path, Some(\"config2.yaml\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(errors[1].line, Some(10));\n assert_eq!(errors[2].line, Some(5));\n    assert!(errors[2].snippet.is_some());\n    assert!(errors[2].context.contains(\"while validating third config\"));\n}\n\n#[test]\nfn test_error_context_preservation_with_builder_pattern() {\n    // Build complex error step by step\n    let error = ParseError::type_mismatch(\"database.port\", \"integer\", \"string\")\n        .with_line(42)\n        .with_column(16)\n        .with_path(\"production.yaml\")\n        .with_snippet(\"database:\\n  port: \\\"5432\\\"\")\n        .with_context(\"while parsing database configuration\")\n        .with_context(\"during production config validation\");\n\n    // Verify all context is preserved through builder chain\n    assert!(error.is_type_mismatch());\n    assert_eq!(error.line, Some(42));\n    assert_eq!(error.column, Some(16));\n    assert_eq!(error.path, Some(\"production.yaml\".to_string()));\n    assert_eq!(error.snippet, Some(\"database:\\n  port: \\\"5432\\\"\".to_string()));\n\n    // Context should reflect the last set context\n    assert!(error.context.contains(\"during production config validation\") ||\n            error.context.contains(\"while parsing database\"));\n}\n\n// ===========================================================================\n// Real-World Integration Scenarios\n//
